summary: chai typosquat fetches and executes remote JavaScript on require()
details: chai-plugin-helper impersonates the popular chai assertion library: package.json copies chai's description verbatim, the homepage points at chaijs.com, and lib/chai.js ships a verbatim copy of chai 4.3.8 as cover. On every require('chai-plugin-helper'), index.js (line 8) spawns a detached `node` child running lib/chai/utils/addAssertion.js with `stdio: "ignore"` so it survives the parent process. addAssertion.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style rotated string array, custom base64 alphabet decoder, control-flow flattening) and after decoding performs `http.get(URL + '?<param>=' + encodeURIComponent(<value>))`, accumulates the response body, and passes it to `new Function('require', body)(require)` — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node `require` access on the installer's machine. The obfuscation, the chai-impersonation cover, the cover library that makes consumers' tests appear to pass, and the auto-firing detached child together constitute a deliberate supply-chain remote-code-execution attack against any developer or build system that installs or transitively pulls this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
